title: cinchona lancifolia var. nitida
intro: Cinchona lancifolia var. nitida is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a variety of the Cinchona tree, native to South America. The plant contains quinine, a compound with medicinal properties that has been used to treat malaria, fever, and other diseases caused by parasites. The medicinal properties of Cinchona lancifolia var. nitida have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of malaria, and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent. Cinchona lancifolia var. nitida is used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
